title: At the margins of the modern state [Book Review]
author: Niesen

opening_move.description: The review opens by noting that Frankfurt School critical theory has come under pressure for neglecting global south perspectives and colonial history, framing Benhabib's collection as an effort to repair this eclipse.
opening_move.stakes_claimed: Cosmopolitan theory must integrate non-western perspectives and colonial history to remain normatively credible

theoretical_positioning.description: The review endorses Benhabib's effort to bring non-western perspectives into cosmopolitan theory, while raising questions about the adequacy of her engagement with the colonial critique.

epistemic_mode.interpretive_methods: close reading of Benhabib and Habermas; postcolonial critique
epistemic_mode.notes: Book review genre with substantial critical engagement. The reviewer brings Habermasian and postcolonial perspectives to bear.

case_type.case_name: Benhabib, At the Margins of the Modern State (2025); Habermas, Between Facts and Norms; Also a History of Philosophy

confidence_notes: Book review genre with substantive critical engagement. The reviewer positions Benhabib's work in relation to both Habermas and postcolonial critiques.
